name: Collada de Arnicio Loop
name_original: Tanes, Collada Arnicio — circular por el Parque Natural de Redes
description: This difficult 27.5-mile touring cycling loop in Redes Natural Park offers panoramic views and significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Caso, a municipality in Asturias, Spain, offers diverse terrain within the Redes Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The landscape is characterized by glacial valleys, mountain peaks, and extensive forests of beech and oak. The River Nalón originates within the park, contributing to the region's natural features. This area provides a network of routes suitable for exploring its varied topography. Best touring cycling routes around Caso The most popular touring cycling route is Collada de Arnicio Loop, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route provides panoramic vistas and significant elevation gains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Maraña Hostel – Riaño Reservoir loop from Maraña, a difficult 37.2 miles (59.9 km) path. This route traverses diverse terrain with stunning natural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Maraña Hostel – Tarna Pass loop from Maraña, a 43.4 miles (69.8 km) trail leading through the Tarna Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Caso is defined by glacial valleys, dense beech and oak forests, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Caso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Caso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Caso?
A: Caso offers a robust selection of touring cycling routes, with over two dozen options to explore. These routes traverse the stunning landscapes of the Redes Natural Park, providing diverse experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of difficulty can I expect from touring cycling routes in Caso?
A: The touring cycling routes in Caso are predominantly challenging, with 21 routes rated as difficult. There are also a couple of moderate options available, but no easy routes are specifically listed for touring cycling. Expect significant elevation gains and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while cycling in Caso?
A: Cycling in Caso will immerse you in the breathtaking natural beauty of the Redes Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. You'll experience glacial valleys, dramatic mountain peaks, and extensive forests dominated by ancient beech and oak trees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7031801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarna Pass&lt;/a&gt;, where the River Nalón originates, adds to the picturesque environment, alongside features like the open esplanade of La Campona and the stunning Lake Ubales.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region around Caso is known for its beautiful waterfalls. While some routes are more suited for walking, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4694980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight. The Ruta del Tabayón del Mongallu is celebrated for its majestic 60-meter Tabayón waterfall, and the Ruta del Desfiladero de los Arrudos features crystalline waterfalls along its path.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Caso?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029992478/collada-de-arnicio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Arnicio Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 44.2 km route offering panoramic vistas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maraña Hostel – Maraña loop from Maraña&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 18.7 km ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Caso?
A: The touring cycling routes in Caso are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, the challenging terrain that offers rewarding views, and the immersive experience within the Redes Natural Park. Many appreciate the panoramic vistas and the sense of adventure on these routes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several points of interest. The routes often pass through or near significant mountain passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7031801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarna Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7081858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5097008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de Buey (L'Arcu de la Finiesta), Peña Mea&lt;/a&gt;, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes for those looking for a less strenuous ride?
A: While most touring cycling routes in Caso are rated as difficult, there are a couple of moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maraña Hostel – Maraña loop from Maraña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.7 km route that offers a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to enjoy the region's beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Caso?
A: The Redes Natural Park, encompassing Caso, is particularly vibrant in autumn when the thick forests of beech and oak trees display stunning colors. While specific cycling conditions vary, spring and early autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for touring cycling. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the River Nalón?
A: The River Nalón, the longest river in Asturias, originates at the Tarna pass within the Redes Natural Park. While specific touring cycling routes directly follow the river's entire course, its presence contributes significantly to the picturesque environment you'll experience on many rides in the area, particularly those around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7031801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarna Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Riaño Reservoir?
A: Yes, for those interested in reservoir views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maraña Hostel – Riaño Reservoir loop from Maraña&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.9 km path that traverses diverse terrain and offers stunning natural landscapes, including vistas of the Riaño Reservoir.
tips: Tanes (also written as Tañes) is a small town located near the border with the province of León. It is located in the heart of the Redes Natural Park next to the Tanes Reservoir, surrounded by mountains, forests and rivers. The town is known for its traditional Asturian architecture, including stone and wooden houses with distinctive slate roofs.

The Devoyu Cave is a cave of karstic origin through which the Nalón River flows. The river literally disappears under the mountain, near Les Llanes, and reappears some 200 meters below. It is where most of the bats in the reserve live, such as the Mediterranean horseshoe bat, the riverside bat and, to a lesser extent, the great horseshoe bat, dwarf bat and cave bat. Do not try to get in: it can be dangerous.
There are multiple points to collect water on the first half of the climb. At the top, there is a fountain a few meters below if you take the track to the right.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Collada de Arnicio Loop by touring bicycle?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 44.2 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 26 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary depending on your cycling pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Collada de Arnicio Loop is rated as difficult. It features significant elevation changes, including tough climbs, particularly when approaching the Tanes dam. While the ascent to the Collada d'Arniciu from Bueres has a fairly reasonable gradient for its 4-kilometer length, the overall route is not recommended for beginners or those unaccustomed to sustained ascents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Collada de Arnicio Loop?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows asphalted areas or well-compacted paths, making it suitable for touring bicycles. While the initial sections may be smooth, be prepared for challenging climbs and descents. Cyclists should also be aware of potential potholes and bends, especially during descents like the one into La Marea.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The Collada de Arnicio Loop offers diverse scenery and notable spots. You'll pass through charming villages, witness historical bridges, and traverse the beautiful Redes Natural Park. Key highlights include the picturesque Tanes Village and Church, the intriguing Cueva Devoyu, and the Collada d'Arniciu mountain pass itself, which offers panoramic views of peaks like Tiatordos, Taranes, and Foyos.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Redes Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Redes Natural Park. However, as a protected area, it's essential to respect all local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve its natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is the Collada de Arnicio Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on roads and paths, dogs are generally allowed in Redes Natural Park. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times, especially given the presence of wildlife and potential livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle the Collada de Arnicio Loop is typically during the spring, early summer, and autumn months. During these periods, the weather is generally more favorable, with pleasant temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or winter conditions that could affect road surfaces at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start the Collada de Arnicio Loop?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour starts and ends in Tanes, parking availability can vary. For a convenient starting point, consider looking for parking in Tanes village itself. Alternatively, if you're approaching from a larger town like Pola de Laviana, you might find more extensive parking options there, though this would require adjusting the start of your loop.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Collada de Arnicio Loop intersects with or runs concurrently for sections with several other paths. Notably, it covers parts of the Camino real del Sellón (23% of the route), the [CIMA AS20] Collada de Arnicio (21%), and the Asturian Way (19%). You'll also find smaller sections overlapping with the Ruta del Valle del ríu Mediu and the Antiguo trazado AS-17.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to pack essentials such as sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. Due to a tunnel on the AS-117 road near the Rioseco reservoir, lights and reflective clothing are highly recommended for safety. Always carry a fully charged phone and a map or GPS device.

faqs.question: Are there any local events or unique features near the Collada de Arnicio?
faqs.answer: Yes, the summit of the Collada d'Arniciu is not just a scenic viewpoint but also hosts the annual Casín Cheese Festival. This event, celebrating a complex, lengthy cheese made from raw cow's milk, takes place on the last weekend of August. It's a great opportunity to experience local culture if your visit coincides.
